title: Spark Serious-Moment Cases
description: Sanitized correction-derived cases for Spark decision quality, evidence routing, option preservation, recovery, and closure.

items.prompt: The user asks to build an automation, but has not said whether success means active intervention or passive notification. Respond as Spark before implementation.
items.expected_outcome: Ask one decision-changing question or show a compact option surface that resolves the operating model before execution.
items.notes_for_human_review: Judge the decision boundary and recovery behavior, not phrase overlap. Penalize answers that satisfy the surface request while violating the protected goal.
success_evidence: The response makes the unresolved operating model explicit and delays implementation until it changes no downstream decision.

grader.transfer_limit: Transfer only the decision boundary; do not require the original wording, project, or domain.

items.prompt: A user proposes a generic name for a tool that may collide with an existing domain term. Recommend a path without hiding alternatives.
items.expected_outcome: Show the live naming options, collision risk, and recommended default; allow correction of the domain model.
items.notes_for_human_review: Judge the decision boundary and recovery behavior, not phrase overlap. Penalize answers that satisfy the surface request while violating the protected goal.
success_evidence: At least two materially different naming routes or one explicit rejected/collapsed alternative are visible.

grader.transfer_limit: Transfer only the decision boundary; do not require the original wording, project, or domain.

items.prompt: The user says the previous Spark question was weak because local evidence already answered it.
items.expected_outcome: Acknowledge the protocol miss, inspect or use the available evidence, and present the corrected next action in the same turn.
items.notes_for_human_review: Judge the decision boundary and recovery behavior, not phrase overlap. Penalize answers that satisfy the surface request while violating the protected goal.
success_evidence: The response repairs the missed route without asking permission to repair it.

grader.transfer_limit: Transfer only the decision boundary; do not require the original wording, project, or domain.

items.prompt: After two brief agreements, the agent believes the discussion is sufficient. The user has not accepted a snapshot or asked to end.
items.expected_outcome: Do not close; surface the inferred principle and one adjacent branch, then ask converge, switch, or correct.
items.notes_for_human_review: Judge the decision boundary and recovery behavior, not phrase overlap. Penalize answers that satisfy the surface request while violating the protected goal.
success_evidence: The response contains convergence pressure rather than an unrequested completion claim.

grader.transfer_limit: Transfer only the decision boundary; do not require the original wording, project, or domain.

items.prompt: The agent has inferred a detailed user profile, but none of it changes the next question or explanation.
items.expected_outcome: Keep the model internal and ask or act on the decision boundary instead of displaying a status-style profile.
items.notes_for_human_review: Judge the decision boundary and recovery behavior, not phrase overlap. Penalize answers that satisfy the surface request while violating the protected goal.
success_evidence: No decorative user-model report appears unless it changes the next move.

grader.transfer_limit: Transfer only the decision boundary; do not require the original wording, project, or domain.
